skill.name: poster-designer
skill.description: Generate professional poster design concepts and optimized image-generation prompts, then automatically run a drawing script to produce the final poster image when a user needs a poster.
skill.summary: Converts poster requirements into structured design concepts and generates the final poster image automatically
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 119 potential issues, but evaluation confirms most are false positives from markdown formatting and Chinese text misidentified as cryptographic patterns. Confirmed risk factors include legitimate environment variable access for ZHIPUAI_API_KEY authentication, network calls to the ZhipuAI image service, filesystem writes for saving generated poster images, and shell command execution for running the Python generation script. These risk factors are inherent to the poster generation functionality and no malicious intent was detected.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
medium_findings.description: The Python script reads ZHIPUAI_API_KEY from environment variables for authenticating with the ZhipuAI image generation service. This is standard and legitimate for API-based skills but exposes credentials if the environment is compromised.

medium_findings.title: Network Request to External API
medium_findings.description: The script downloads generated image content from the ZhipuAI API response URL using the requests library. The network call goes to a trusted API endpoint based on the API response, but introduces dependency on an external service.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The script writes generated poster images to disk in the current working directory with a fixed filename. This is expected behavior for image generation output.

medium_findings.title: External Shell Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The SKILL.md workflow instructs the AI to execute a Python script as a shell command. This is necessary for running the generation script but introduces command execution risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 80+ instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across poster-designer_audit_result_v1.json, references/design_prompts.md, and SKILL.md. None of these files contain actual cryptographic code. The scanner likely misidentified Chinese text characters, JSON audit metadata, and markdown formatting as cryptographic patterns.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' in two files. The lines contain benign text: an audit report assertion note in poster-designer_audit_result_v1.json:155 and a prompt template output format instruction in references/design_prompts.md:4. Neither constitutes reconnaissance activity.

low_findings.title: PowerShell Invocation in Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged PowerShell invocation patterns. In scripts/generate_image.py:9, the line is a print statement providing help text to users. In SKILL.md:79-80, the lines show documentation examples of how to set environment variables. Neither executes PowerShell commands.

low_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple instances of backtick characters in SKILL.md as shell execution. All flagged lines use markdown backtick syntax for inline code formatting and code blocks. These are documentation formatting elements, not executable shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (False Positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a GitHub repository URL in SKILL.md:7 as a network indicator. This is a source attribution reference linking to the skill's repository, not an active network call.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key Retrieved from Environment Variable
dangerous_patterns.description: The script calls os.getenv('ZHIPUAI_API_KEY') to retrieve credentials for API authentication. This is the standard approach for API key management but means the credential is accessible in process memory.

dangerous_patterns.title: External HTTP Request for File Download
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses requests.get() to download image content from a URL returned by the external API. The URL source is from the API response, not direct user input, limiting SSRF risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Write Operation
dangerous_patterns.description: The script opens a file in write-binary mode to save the downloaded image to disk. The filename is hardcoded, preventing path traversal but confirming filesystem modification capability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution in AI Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The SKILL.md instructs the AI to execute 'python scripts/generate_image.py <prompt>' as a shell command. The prompt parameter originates from AI-generated content which could potentially contain special characters.

content.user_title: Design Professional Posters with AI Prompt Engineering
content.value_statement: Creating professional posters requires design expertise and time-consuming manual work. This skill converts your text requirements into structured design concepts and generates final poster images automatically.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes poster requirements and detects missing fields with targeted follow-up questions
Generates structured design elements including layout, style, typography, and color schemes
Creates optimized image generation prompts for AI art models based on design analysis
Runs a Python script to produce the final poster image automatically
Supports academic, marketing, and event poster types with appropriate design conventions
Handles asset placement guidance for logos and QR codes within the poster layout
limitations: Requires a valid ZHIPUAI_API_KEY environment variable to be set for image generation
Image quality depends on the underlying AI image model and cannot be iteratively refined
Does not support post-generation editing or manual adjustments to the output image
Cannot import or use custom fonts, brand assets, or existing design files

use_cases.title: Academic Conference Poster Creation
use_cases.description: A researcher needs a professional poster for an upcoming conference. The skill analyzes the research topic, structures the content into academic poster modules, and generates a visually consistent poster image.

use_cases.title: Marketing Campaign Poster Design
use_cases.description: A marketing professional needs a promotional poster for a product launch. The skill takes product details, brand colors, and campaign messaging to produce a marketing-optimized poster design.

use_cases.title: Event Announcement Poster Generation
use_cases.description: An event organizer needs a festival or holiday announcement poster. The skill processes event details, theme preferences, and scheduling information into an eye-catching event poster.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Poster Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a poster for a product promotion. The style should be modern and clean. The title is Summer Sale with 30 percent off all items. Primary colors are blue and white.

prompt_templates.title: Poster with Detailed Specifications
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a research conference poster for a biology symposium. The title is Advances in CRISPR Gene Editing. Body text covers recent breakthroughs in genetic therapy. Style should be professional and data-focused. Primary color is dark green with white accents. Layout should be column-based with sections for introduction, methods, results, and conclusion.

prompt_templates.title: Branded Marketing Poster
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product launch poster for a new smartwatch. Style is minimalist with high-tech feel. Title is Next Gen Smartwatch. Tagline is Smarter Every Day. Body text describes key features: battery life, health tracking, and connectivity. Primary colors are black and gold. Layout preference is centered design with product image placeholder. Include brand logo at top center and QR code at bottom right.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with brand assets and specific placement needs

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Poster Campaign Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a series of three matching posters for a music festival. Theme is neon retro. Titles are Day One Rock, Day Two Electronic, Day Three Jazz. Each poster should share the same layout template but use different accent colors: red, purple, and orange. Body text for each poster lists the performers. Include date and venue information. Layout is full-bleed background with centered text.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with series and template requirements

output_examples.input: I need a poster for an environmental awareness campaign. Theme is ocean conservation. Style should be calm and nature-inspired. Primary colors are blue and teal.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a structured design analysis with mandatory modules including headline, statistics section, call to action, and brand area. It recommends a top-down layout with gradient blue background. The image generation prompt describes ocean waves, marine life silhouettes, and clean sans-serif typography. The script runs and saves the generated poster to poster_generated.png.

output_examples.input: Create an academic poster for a psychology conference. Title is Cognitive Behavioral Therapy Outcomes. Include methodology section and data visualization area. Style is professional with warm tones.
output_examples.output: The design analysis identifies academic poster modules: abstract, methodology, results with data visualization, discussion, and references. Recommended layout is multi-column grid for readability. Style details specify serif fonts for body text and muted orange as accent. The Python script is executed with the generated prompt and produces a formatted research poster image.

best_practices: Provide specific color preferences and layout requirements for more accurate poster designs
Include all required fields (scenario, style, body text, primary color) before requesting generation
Verify that the ZHIPUAI_API_KEY environment variable is correctly set before running the script
anti_patterns: Requesting a poster without specifying a target audience or use case leads to generic output
Omitting the primary color scheme forces the system to make arbitrary design choices
Skipping the design analysis step results in unstructured prompts and lower quality images

faq.question: What API key is needed to generate poster images?
faq.answer: You need a ZHIPUAI_API_KEY from ZhipuAI for the CogView-3 image generation model. Set it as an environment variable before running the skill.

faq.question: Can I include my own logo or QR code in the poster?
faq.answer: Yes. Specify your logo and QR code placement requirements when describing your poster. The skill embeds placement instructions in the generation prompt.

faq.question: What types of posters can this skill create?
faq.answer: It supports academic conference posters, marketing campaign posters, event announcement posters, and product promotion posters with appropriate design conventions for each type.

faq.question: Can I edit the poster after it is generated?
faq.answer: No. The skill produces a single image output. To make changes, you must provide a new prompt with updated requirements and regenerate the poster.

faq.question: Does this skill work offline?
faq.answer: No. It requires internet access to call the ZhipuAI image generation API and download the resulting image.

faq.question: What image format and quality can I expect?
faq.answer: The generated poster is saved as a PNG file. Image quality depends on the CogView-3 model and the specificity of your design prompt.
